description: Investigating bus priority and pedestrian safety improvements on Flatbush Avenue between Tillary Street and Avenue V. Flatbush Avenue is a future bus priority corridor from the NYC/MTA Transit Improvement Summit, a priority corridor in the NYC Streets Plan, and a Vision Zero priority corridor.

Proposal for center-running bus lanes, bus boarding islands, curb management, and pedestrian safety improvements on Flatbush Avenue from Livingston Street to Grand Army Plaza, Brooklyn.
Center-running physically protected bus lanes on Flatbush Avenue between State Street and Grand Army Plaza, with painted pedestrian spaces, parking/loading updates, and Pacific Street reversal
Proposed center-running bus lanes, bus boarding islands, curb management, bus stop consolidation, and route changes on Flatbush Avenue between Dean Street and Grand Army Plaza
Proposal for center-running bus lanes on Flatbush Avenue from Livingston St to Grand Army Plaza, with future phases to study other sections

Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue in Brooklyn, focusing on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section in current phase. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes.

Current round of design options focuses on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush corridor section. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes. DOT plans to study bus priority treatments in other sections in future phases.
Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue in Brooklyn, led by NYCDOT and MTA, with community advisory board process

Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue in Brooklyn, launched with a kickoff meeting on June 30, 2022. Part of the next round of bus priority projects announced at the Transit Improvement Summit on June 16, 2022.
Bus priority improvements on Flatbush Avenue including curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes. Current round focuses on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section.

Bus priority improvements on Flatbush Avenue focusing on Downtown Brooklyn/Northern Flatbush section. Three design options being studied: curbside bus lanes, offset bus lanes, and center-running bus lanes.

Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue, a critical cross-borough transportation corridor in Brooklyn. Part of the Transit Improvement Summit announced June 16, 2022 by Mayor Eric Adams and MTA Chair Janno Lieber.
Center-running bus lanes on Flatbush Avenue from Livingston St to Grand Army Plaza, with future phases to study other sections

Bus priority project on Flatbush Avenue from Tillary Street to Kings Plaza through downtown Brooklyn to southern Brooklyn. A collaborative effort between NYC DOT and MTA to improve transit service, including bus lanes, busways, automated enforcement, and transit signal priority.
